message:
feat(doctor): add performance benchmarks for xf doctor (xf-11.4.4)

Adds comprehensive performance benchmarking to the doctor command:

- benchmark_index_load: Measures Tantivy index open time (3 iterations)
- benchmark_simple_query: Tests single-word query latency (5 queries x 10 iterations)
- benchmark_phrase_query: Tests quoted phrase query latency
- benchmark_complex_query: Tests boolean query latency (AND, OR, NOT)
- benchmark_fts5_query: Tests SQLite FTS5 query latency via search_tweets
- run_performance_benchmarks: Master function that runs all benchmarks

Features:
- LatencyStats struct with min/max/mean/p50/p95/p99 percentiles
- Configurable thresholds for pass/warning status
- Human-readable output format (e.g., "p50=1.2ms, p95=3.4ms, p99=5.6ms (n=50)")
- Actionable suggestions for slow queries (optimize index, use SSD)

Thresholds:
- Query latency: <50ms acceptable, <100ms warning, >=100ms high
- Index load: <500ms acceptable, <1000ms warning, >=1000ms very slow

Closes xf-11.4.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
